title: P1.6 — Hero visual: pick the tier
footer_text: Storyweave P1.6 · the three comps are throwaway Artifacts; your pick gets built into the live home hero at O5 and ships at O7 after a real Lighthouse/perf gate. Axis defaults are listed in the recommendation — note any change in the comment.

sitrep.gate_purpose: Pick the rendering tier for the home hero network visual — approve the T2 recommendation, or amend to T1 or T3.
sitrep.importance_reason: sets what gets built into the live home hero (O5) and deployed (O7)

decision_title: Approve T2 (Constellation) as the production hero visual
question: Recommend shipping T2 — the Canvas-2D constellation — as the home hero network visual: it reads best-in-class (glowing orbs, flowing relationship arcs, breathing core, forming seeds), needs no heavy dependency, and stays in the perf budget. Open the three live comps side-by-side, then APPROVE to build T2 into the site (O5), or AMEND to ship T1 or T3 instead — name which, plus any axis changes, in the comment.  ·  LIVE COMPS (open each; T3's WebGL bloom needs your real GPU):  T1 Refined-SVG → https://claude.ai/code/artifact/12a5007a-b75e-4b33-aab4-283a76099d07   ·   T2 Constellation (recommended) → https://claude.ai/code/artifact/40c27fc0-34cb-46f2-9fee-bd46eeb58526   ·   T3 Deep-Field-WebGL → https://claude.ai/code/artifact/792784da-f4aa-46a0-b073-fb78bfa97983

strengths: Reads best-in-class — a living network, not a diagram
Stays in the perf budget (no heavy bundle)
No dependency — hand-rolled Canvas, evolves the north star
Ships on the SSR-SVG no-JS + a11y floor (T1)
weaknesses: Hand-rolled canvas is more to maintain than SVG
Needs an a11y text-twin + SSR baseline behind it
opportunities: Hover-doorway turns the hero into an entry to /vaults
threats: Motion could outrun the honest register (Movement-Skeptic)
Canvas cost on low-end mobile — cap DPR/particles

recommendation.rationale: T2 is the sweet spot: the operator-approved constellation feeling, best-in-class, with no heavy dependency and headroom against gate-10/19. T1 is the guaranteed-safe baseline and doubles as the no-JS/a11y floor every tier ships on; T3 (WebGL) raises the ceiling with GPU bloom + depth but carries real bundle/perf risk to be measured at O6. Build one, keep T1 as the floor.
key_factors: T2 hits the 10 design rules; T1 = floor, T3 = ceiling-check
Perf is the load-bearing risk and it bites only for T3
All tiers keep the SSR-SVG no-JS + a11y twin (honest floor)
Axis defaults: ambient + hover-doorway · honest forming-seeds · shared graph language · keep the dimmed pixel-art ground
risks: T3-if-picked: WebGL bundle can blow gate-10/19 — measured at O6 with a real Lighthouse run before ship
Any tier: the a11y text-twin + no-JS baseline are non-negotiable
next_actions: APPROVE → build T2 into HomeHero.astro at O5 (SSR baseline + Canvas enhancement + a11y twin)
AMEND → say T1 or T3 (and any axis change) in the comment; I rebuild the O5 plan to match
Either way → O6 gates + real Lighthouse, then the O7 ship-gate before deploy
